events.title: Rancho Readers Book Discussion Group
events.description:
This casual "round robin" discussion group meets in our Library’s Program Room every fourth Tuesday of the month, 5:30-6:30pm. 



Browse our Rancho Readers display or search our Catalog using keywords to find any fiction or nonfiction title in the current theme. Then, after borrowing and enjoying your selection, join us so we can all share what we read! 



Contact us with any questions at 949-459-6094 or email ocpl.rsm@occr.ocgov.com



Click on PDF below for the 2026 Schedule of Reading & Discussion Themes.

events.title: Cookbook Author: Jet Tila
events.description:
Join us at the Aliso Viejo Library for a brief cooking demonstration with chef Jet Tila! Jet Tila is a culinary trailblazer, best-selling author, and expert in modern Asian cuisine. He has appeared on numerous Food Network shows including Chopped, Iron Chef America, Cutthroat Kitchen, and Guy's Grocery Games, and has served as a judge and competitor. Jet owns several restaurants across the U.S. and has co-authored cookbooks such as 101 Asian Dishes You Need to Cook Before You Die. Known for blending tradition with innovation, Jet brings energy, precision, and cultural depth to every dish.

Limited complimentary books available, while supplies last. No registration required, all ages welcome. Early arrival suggested.

This project is funded in part through a grant from the California Department of Aging and administered locally by the Orange County Office on Aging.

events.title: One County, One Book: Music Appreciation Performance
events.display_title: [FREE] One County, One Book: Music Appreciation Performance
events.description:
Celebrate literature through music at a special live performance by acclaimed pianist Jericho Law, inspired by this year's One County, One Book selection, James by Percival Everett. This project is funded in part through a grant from the California Department of Aging and administered locally by the Orange County Office on Aging.

events.description: Junk journaling is a creative and flexible way to preserve memories and reflect on your life using handmade pages crafted from recycled or found materials like ticket stubs, menus, programs, magazine clippings, and more! Bring your junk journals, craft supplies, and cherished mementos, and join fellow journalers for a relaxed and inspiring session. On these nights, we dim the lights in the museum, turn off the sounds, and offer a limited capacity of 250 guests. Staff are happy and ready to assist with providing various materials, including noise-cancelling headphones, fidget toys, sunglasses, and weighted materials for your convenience.

Every second Tuesday of the month

5:30-6:30 PM

Discover a new appreciation for poetry in a comfortable but inspiring group setting!

During each meeting, we focus on a suggested poetry theme, style, or mood with a brief recital or presentation, writing time with creative prompts, and a positive ‘share and discuss’ time.

          2026 Schedule of Themes:

          1/13: “Thresholds”

          2/10: “Reflections”

          3/10: “Whispers”

          4/14: “Seedlings”

          5/12: “Journeys”

          6/9: “Harmonics“

          7/14: “Horizons”

          8/11: “Imagination”

          9/8: “Illumination”

         10/13: “Shadows”

         11/10: “Memories”

         12/8: “Constellations”



         Meetings are in the Program Room, 5:30-6:30 PM.  Writing and discussion prompts will be shared.

events.description:
Get messy. Create together.
Our immersive summer art exhibit invites families to paint, sculpt, build, and explore without limits. This summer, art isn’t something you look at—it’s something to experience together. Every visit brings something new, where creativity grows through art, movement, and play.
10 am – 3:30 pm
This exhibit is included in museum admission. Space is limited! Book your tickets now.

*Events and dates are subject to change.
Pretend City Summer Hours
Open Tuesday – Sunday
–Closed on Mondays except Memorial Day and Labor Day
–Closed on Father’s Day and Fourth of July

Frequently Asked Questions
Is the summer exhibit included with admission?Yes. The exhibit is included with regular museum admission.
Will there be special programming?Yes. Additional programming and special activities will be announced soon.
What ages is this exhibit best for?The exhibit is designed for children ages 2–9 and their families.
Do children need art experience to participate?No. This is an open-ended, play-based experience where children can explore creativity at their own pace.
Will it be messy?Yes. This exhibit encourages hands-on creativity, so we recommend dressing children in clothes that can get messy.
Are you open on Mondays?No. Pretend City is closed on Mondays, except for Memorial Day and Labor Day.
Why does the exhibit close early?The exhibit closes at 3:30 pm to allow time for cleaning and resetting the space. The museum remains open until 4:00 pm.

events.title: Water Safety with Big Blue Swim School
events.display_title: Water Safety with Big Blue Swim School
events.description:
Introducing the importance of water safety to our citizens!
TIME: 11 am ONLY
About the Play Partner 
Big Blue Swim School teaches kids ages 3 months to 12 years how to be safe, happy, and confident swimmers. Learning to swim is about becoming safer in the water and unlocking the potential of each child. In our warm, friendly environment, professional staff teach the foundational elements of swimming so kids can swim farther and farther independently. Along the way, kids learn about themselves, discover how to build confidence, engage in curiosity, and develop resilience to live a bigger, bolder, brighter life.

events.title: Neurodivergent Family Night
events.description:
Join us for a free evening where children and caretakers are invited to play in a sensory-friendly, neuro-affirming environment! We encourage the whole family to attend, and therapists can join as well.
Neurodivergent Family Nights are held on the second Tuesday and fourth Thursday of each month from 5-7 pm (after we close to general admission).
On these nights, we dim the lights in the museum, turn off the sounds, and offer a limited capacity of 250 guests. Staff are happy and ready to assist with providing various materials, including noise-cancelling headphones, fidget toys, sunglasses, and weighted materials for your convenience.
RSVP COMING SOON
